Overview

Address
631 Caring Street, Hillman, MI 49746
Phone
9897424581
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
36 (93% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1982-06-22
Setting
Rural
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.52 3.75 4.65 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.20 0.66 1.23 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.74 0.83 0.76 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.58 2.26 2.66 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.94 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.71
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.56
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.82
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
35.9%
annual
RN turnover
36.4%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.33
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-11 6 5 1 72 1 72
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-09 14 7 7 84 1 84
Total weighted health score
75.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
2
last cycle

Deficiencies (25)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

22 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-09-11 2025-10-07
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2025-09-11 2025-10-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-09-11 2025-10-07
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-09-11 2025-10-07
0923 Have enough outside ventilation via a window or mechanical ventilation, or both. F 2025-09-11 2025-10-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-03-12 2025-03-28
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-10-09 2024-11-04
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-02-29 2024-03-21
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-02-29 2024-03-21
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-02-29 2024-03-21
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-02-29 2024-03-21
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-02-29 2024-03-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-10-19 2023-11-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-10-19 2023-11-20
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. F 2023-10-19 2023-11-20
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2023-10-19 2023-11-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-10-19 2023-11-20
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-06-09 2023-07-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-06-09 2023-07-12
